Scenario: Fresh install loads expected defaults
Preconditions: App newly installed in test subreddit
Test Steps: 1) Install app 2) Open inline post + mod panel 3) Check default settings/templates/theme
Expected Result: Default values are present and app loads without errors

Scenario: Settings are isolated per subreddit
Preconditions: App installed in Sub A and Sub B
Test Steps: 1) In Sub A set unique modmail subject/theme/template 2) Open Sub B mod panel
Expected Result: Sub B remains unchanged and does not inherit Sub A settings

Scenario: History/blocked records isolated per subreddit
Preconditions: Same user submits in Sub A only
Test Steps: 1) Submit + process verification in Sub A 2) Open Sub B history/blocked/pending
Expected Result: No records appear in Sub B for Sub A actions

Scenario: Only moderators can save settings
Preconditions: Test with mod user and non-mod user
Test Steps: 1) As non-mod open app 2) Attempt settings/template actions 3) Repeat as mod
Expected Result: Non-mod receives permission denial; mod can save

Scenario: Disabled verifications block new submissions
Test Steps: 1) Turn off verifications in mod settings 2) As user attempt submit
Expected Result: Submission blocked with configured disabled message

Scenario: Required photo count = 1 enforces one image
Test Steps: 1) Open submit form 2) Verify one image field shown 3) Submit with one image
Expected Result: Submission succeeds with one required image

Scenario: Required photo count = 2 enforces two images
Test Steps: 1) Open submit form 2) Verify two image fields shown 3) Try with one image then two
Expected Result: One image blocked; two images accepted

Scenario: Required photo count = 3 enforces three images
Test Steps: 1) Open submit form 2) Verify three image fields shown 3) Try with two then three
Expected Result: Two images blocked; three images accepted

Scenario: Successful submission creates pending + latest
Test Steps: 1) Submit verification 2) Refresh dashboard
Expected Result: User appears in pending queue and latest status is PENDING

Scenario: Resubmission does not create duplicate active records
Test Steps: 1) Submit verification 2) Submit again 3) Open pending/history
Expected Result: Only newest active pending record remains; no duplicate active entry

Scenario: Blocked user cannot submit
Test Steps: 1) Block user in mod panel 2) Login as blocked user 3) Attempt submit
Expected Result: Submission blocked and button/action prevented as designed

Scenario: Approve updates status and queue
Preconditions: At least one pending record exists
Test Steps: 1) Click Approve 2) Refresh panel
Expected Result: Record leaves pending queue and appears approved

Scenario: Approve applies flair
Test Steps: 1) Approve pending user 2) Check user flair in subreddit
Expected Result: Flair applied successfully (template or fallback path)

Scenario: Approve writes modmail + mod note
Test Steps: 1) Approve user 2) Check modmail thread 3) Check mod notes
Expected Result: Modmail posted and mod note added; per-step status reported in UI

Scenario: Deny requires denial reason
Test Steps: 1) Click Deny without reason 2) Retry with reason
Expected Result: Without reason blocked; with reason action proceeds

Scenario: Deny updates status and removes from pending
Test Steps: 1) Deny with reason 2) Refresh panel
Expected Result: Record leaves pending and status is DENIED

Scenario: Deny includes moderator notes in message
Test Steps: 1) Enter denial reason + moderator notes 2) Deny 3) Inspect modmail
Expected Result: Reason and notes appear in denial modmail body

Scenario: Denial counter increments and auto-blocks at threshold
Test Steps: 1) Deny same user until threshold reached 2) Check blocked tab
Expected Result: User auto-blocked when threshold reached; UI message indicates block

Scenario: Repeated approve/deny click does not double-apply
Test Steps: 1) Double-click action quickly or retry action 2) Check audit/history
Expected Result: No duplicate final actions for same verification record

Scenario: Pending updates reuse same modmail thread per user
Test Steps: 1) Submit pending action for same user multiple times 2) Inspect modmail
Expected Result: Replies go to existing thread; no duplicate new thread creation

Scenario: Single modmail subject used for action notifications
Test Steps: 1) Configure subject with {{subreddit}} 2) Trigger approve/deny/remove
Expected Result: Same configured subject used; placeholder renders correctly

Scenario: Action header appears at top of message body
Test Steps: 1) Trigger each action 2) Inspect resulting modmail body
Expected Result: Header appears as bold text followed by underline separator

Scenario: All placeholders render correctly in templates
Test Steps: 1) Use {{username}} {{mod}} {{subreddit}} {{date submitted}} {{reason}} {{days}} 2) Trigger actions
Expected Result: Placeholders resolve to expected values in outgoing messages

Scenario: Moderator removal of approved verification works
Preconditions: At least one approved user exists
Test Steps: 1) Click Remove Verification with reason 2) Refresh panel
Expected Result: Status becomes REMOVED and reason is recorded/audited

Scenario: Moderator removal removes flair
Test Steps: 1) Remove verification by moderator 2) Check user flair
Expected Result: Flair removed; failure shown explicitly if removal fails

Scenario: Withdraw pending request removes pending only
Test Steps: 1) User clicks withdraw pending 2) Refresh dashboard
Expected Result: Pending request removed and no approval/denial side effects

Scenario: Delete verification data removes flair and user data
Test Steps: 1) User runs delete/remove verification 2) Refresh 3) Check flair
Expected Result: User data removed and flair removed (or explicit failure surfaced)

Scenario: Search by username returns correct records
Test Steps: 1) Search by username 2) Compare against known actions
Expected Result: Only matching user records returned

Scenario: Search by moderator returns correct records
Preconditions: Have records moderated by different mods
Test Steps: 1) Search by moderator name 2) Verify results
Expected Result: Only records reviewed by that moderator returned

Scenario: Search by date range filters correctly
Test Steps: 1) Search with from/to dates 2) Validate inclusions/exclusions
Expected Result: Results respect date window boundaries

Scenario: Pagination/Load more works without duplicates
Test Steps: 1) Run broad search 2) Click Load More multiple times
Expected Result: Results append correctly with no duplicates/skips

Scenario: Manual purge older than 90 days only removes eligible history
Test Steps: 1) Run purge older than 90 days 2) Re-check history
Expected Result: Only records older than 90 days are removed; recent records stay

Scenario: Purge does not alter flair/block state
Preconditions: Have users with active flair and blocked users
Test Steps: 1) Capture pre-state (flair/blocked) 2) Run purge 3) Compare
Expected Result: No unintended flair/block/verification status changes from purge

Scenario: Audit-only purge respects minimum age
Preconditions: Have audit entries newer and older than threshold
Test Steps: 1) Run audit purge menu action 2) Review audit list
Expected Result: Only old audit entries removed; recent entries remain

Scenario: Theme preset applies across mod panel and inline UI
Test Steps: 1) Save preset 2) Refresh mod panel and inline post
Expected Result: Consistent colors/tokens applied in both surfaces

Scenario: Custom primary/accent override works with validation
Test Steps: 1) Set valid hex values and save 2) Try invalid values
Expected Result: Valid values apply; invalid values rejected with fallback/validation

Scenario: Dark/light mode respected per user preference
Test Steps: 1) Toggle OS/browser theme 2) Reload app
Expected Result: UI selects matching light/dark token set and remains readable

Scenario: No incorrect initial theme flash on panel load
Test Steps: 1) Hard refresh mod panel 2) Observe initial paint
Expected Result: Panel loads directly with resolved theme (no dark/default flash)

Scenario: Per-step errors are explicit and non-silent
Test Steps: modmail)
Expected Result: 1) Trigger approve/deny/remove while inducing failure 2) Observe toast/state
Pass/Fail: UI shows step-level success/fail reason for flair/modmail/mod note

Scenario: Approve/Deny action latency acceptable
Test Steps: 1) Time click-to-feedback for approve/deny 2) Repeat 5x
Expected Result: UI responds quickly and dashboard refreshes without stale state

Scenario: Block and unblock flow works end-to-end
Test Steps: 1) Block user 2) Confirm blocked list entry 3) Unblock
Expected Result: User appears then is removed from blocked list correctly

Scenario: Storage metrics render and update after actions
Test Steps: 1) Note storage stats 2) Perform actions 3) Refresh
Expected Result: Storage estimate and breakdown update without errors

Scenario: Validation job remains scheduled per subreddit
Test Steps: 1) Trigger install/upgrade path in test 2) Check scheduler logs/events
Expected Result: Daily validation schedule exists and runs for that subreddit scope

Scenario: Validation cleanup does not delete active valid users
Test Steps: 1) Run/replay validation job 2) Compare approved list before/after
Expected Result: Valid active users remain; only invalid/deleted/suspended users are purged

Scenario: Existing installs load new template fields safely
Test Steps: 1) Upgrade app build 2) Open templates tab 3) Save once
Expected Result: Legacy values are read (fallback) and save succeeds under new fields

Scenario: Full user lifecycle from submit to cleanup
Test Steps: 1) Submit 2) Approve 3) Remove by mod 4) User resubmits 5) Deny 6) Block/unblock
Expected Result: All transitions and UI states behave correctly without data corruption
